meaning: n.浮货,废料,零碎物,流离失所者
examples: The water was full of flotsam and refuse.
The beaches are wide and filled with interesting flotsam and jetsam.
Memories, emotions, conditioning and all sorts of other mental flotsam taint every new experience you gain.
Google (founded in 1998) makes sure you pull up less flotsam when you search.
These kids scoured the shore for flotsam and jetsam to play with, bring home or eat.
